mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth, forcing the Fed to maintain restrictive policy longer, which compresses equity multiples and drags down forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when UNRATE sustains below 4.5% for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit expansion combined with rising inflation signals overheating demand that triggers aggressive monetary tightening, leading to valuation compression and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive SPX returns occur in more than 50% of observations where RSAFS and CPIAUCSL both rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained environment where industrial production growth decelerates while inflation remains elevated, supply bottlenecks erode corporate margins, causing negative forward equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO decelerates below 0.5% MoM while CPIAUCSL remains above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and construction activity, which historically lead positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as economic momentum translates into near-term earnings revisions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with HOUST increases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.5%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to dampen corporate capex and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when FEDFUNDS sustains above 0.5% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and consumer credit indicates robust aggregate demand expansion, which historically drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon as earnings growth accelerates.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of periods where both PAYEMS and RSAFS show positive month-over-month changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid real GDP expansion above 3% annualized signals late-cycle overheating that historically precedes equity drawdowns as valuation multiples contract under inflation fears over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 3%.
